learn.title: Floating Offshore Wind Platforms
learn.intro: Floating offshore wind unlocks vast deep-water wind resources unreachable by fixed foundations. With stronger, steadier winds far from shore, it can power millions of homes and cut emissions. The US DOE advances R&D to lower costs and scale this key technology for the clean energy transition.
learn.c1_text: Fixed turbines work in water under 60 m deep; floating platforms use mooring lines and anchors to stay stable in depths over 60 m. Like a buoy tethered to the seabed, they harness stronger winds offshore where 80% of US wind resource lies.
learn.c2_text: Semisubmersible, spar-buoy and tension-leg platforms balance turbines using ballast and mooring. Semisubmersibles use pontoons; spars extend deep like a fishing float; tension legs pull taut to limit motion. Each suits different depths and wave conditions for stable power generation.
learn.c3_text: India targets 30 GW offshore wind by 2030, with floating pilots off Tamil Nadu and Gujarat. Although most planned projects are fixed-bottom, deep-water floating R&D aligns with MNRE's roadmap to tap India's 140 GW offshore wind potential.
learn.stat1: US offshore wind pipeline exceeded 40 GW in 2023
learn.stat2: 80% of US offshore wind resource in deep water
learn.stat3: Floating turbines viable beyond 60 m depth

learn.title: India Green Hydrogen Mission
learn.intro: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission drives clean fuel for industry and transport, cutting fossil imports. With ₹19,744 crore outlay, it targets 5 MMT annual production by 2030, boosting energy security and exports while creating green jobs across the renewable sector.
learn.c1_text: The mission aims 5 MMT green hydrogen yearly by 2030, needing 125 GW renewable capacity. Like swapping coal for sunlight in making fuel, it decarbonizes refineries and fertilizer. This cuts 50 MMT CO2 emissions and saves ₹1 lakh crore in imports annually.
learn.c2_text: MNRE offers viability gap funding for electrolyzers and pilots. Producers get demand aggregation via mandates on refineries. Like subsidies that scaled solar, these de-risk early projects, targeting below $2/kg hydrogen cost through domestic manufacturing and scale.
learn.c3_text: NTPC and Reliance plan GW-scale plants in Gujarat and Rajasthan. MNRE's SIGHT scheme supports 450,000 tonnes electrolyzer capacity by 2025. India's cheap renewables make it a future export hub to EU and Japan under bilateral green ammonia pacts.

learn.title: Iron-Air Long Duration Batteries
learn.intro: Iron-air batteries store renewable energy for 100+ hours, bridging multi-day gaps when sun and wind fade. Costing a fraction of lithium, they stabilize grids with cheap abundant iron. US DOE backs them as key long-duration storage for a reliable clean grid.
learn.c1_text: Iron-air cells breathe oxygen to rust iron during discharge, reversing on charge. Like iron nails oxidizing in water, the reaction stores energy cheaply. Using abundant iron and air, systems cost ~$20/kWh, versus $150 for lithium, for 100-hour discharge.
learn.c2_text: Unlike 4-hour lithium, iron-air delivers 100+ hours. Paired with solar, a 1 MW unit powers 1000 homes for days during calm clouds. DOE funds pilots to prove 90% round-trip efficiency at utility scale by 2030 for grid resilience.
learn.c3_text: India needs 236 GWh storage by 2032 for 500 GW renewables. While lithium dominates now, iron-air could cut costs for DISCOMs in Rajasthan balancing solar peaks. Indian firms explore local iron-based chemistries under national storage mission guidelines.

learn.title: Small Wind Turbines
learn.intro: Small wind turbines under 100 kW power homes, farms and villages off-grid. They cut diesel use and bills using local wind. US DOE shows how smart siting and net metering make clean distributed wind accessible for communities and rural energy independence.
learn.c1_text: Blades spin a generator from wind over 3 m/s, like a pinwheel charging a phone. A 5 kW turbine serves a home, making 10,000 kWh yearly in good sites. Inverters sync to batteries or grid, reducing grid power by 50% for rural users.
learn.c2_text: Turbines need clear 10 m above trees at 5x height. DOE tools map wind speed; 5 m/s average yields payback in 8 years. Net metering sells excess to utility, like rooftop solar, boosting community microgrids and resilience during outages.
learn.c3_text: India's MNRE promotes 1-25 kW turbines for 10,000 unelectrified hamlets. Gujarat dairy co-ops use 10 kW units cutting diesel 70%. With 6 m/s coastal winds, small wind hybrids with solar cut rural subsidy burden under Saubhagya scheme.

learn.title: Geothermal Energy Basics
learn.intro: Geothermal taps Earth's heat for 24/7 power and heating with tiny land use. Unlike intermittent solar, it runs constantly, decarbonizing grids and districts. US DOE basics show enhanced systems could power 40 million homes, vital for stable clean transition.
learn.c1_text: Heat from radioactive decay in crust reaches 300°C at 5 km depth. Like a kettle underground, wells extract steam to spin turbines. A 50 MW plant serves 50,000 homes continuously, with 99% uptime versus 25% for wind, using 1 km² versus solar farms.
learn.c2_text: Binary plants transfer heat to fluid boiling at 100°C for generation. District heating pipes hot water to buildings, cutting gas use 80%. DOE notes 3 km wells unlock 100 GW US potential via enhanced geothermal stimulation of hot dry rock.
learn.c3_text: India's Himalaya and Cambay basin hold 10 GW potential per GSI. Ladakh pilots 1 MW heating for monasteries. MNRE includes geothermal in renewable mix; Puga springs could power 5,000 homes, reducing diesel in border villages by 60%.

learn.title: Wave Energy Converters
learn.intro: Wave energy converters harvest ocean motion for predictable coastal power. With global potential 29,000 TWh, they complement wind and solar. US DOE researches devices to cut costs and power blue economy islands, key for resilient ocean-based renewables.
learn.c1_text: Point absorbers bob like buoys; oscillating water columns trap waves pushing air through turbines; attenuators snake with swells. A 1 MW absorber yields 3 GWh yearly in 2 m waves. DOE tests show 20-year life in harsh seas with 30% efficiency.
learn.c2_text: Mooring and corrosion limit output; power take-off converts slow motion to 60 Hz grid power. Like gears on a bicycle, hydraulic rams boost speed. DOE's PacWave site measures 500 kW devices, targeting $0.50/kWh by 2035 from $1.50 today.
learn.c3_text: India's 7,500 km coast holds 40 GW wave potential per NIOT. Tamil Nadu pilots 100 kW converters near fishing hamlets. Under MNRE, Vizhinjam test bed supports devices cutting diesel for islands by 50%, aiding Andaman clean energy goals.
